game.title: EyeOS
game.short_text: Find your lost friend by snooping through their Apple II inspired computer.

details.title: EyeOS
details.description:
A short, queer (and borderline nonsensical) mystery game where you are trying to find out what happened to your missing friend by rooting around in their computer. Play by clicking on things and searching terms in the EyeOS home screen! Note: try to keep search terms concise, and *don't press enter*---it'll search on its own, and hitting enter will disrupt results. Also I figured it would be played in one sitting, so it does not currently have save states (but I can add them if people want).
Submission to the Safe In our World Game Dev Champions game jam, solo over 25 category.

Find out why!
This game is concerned with anxiety, dysphoria, and isolation. It discusses these topics lightheartedly but with sincerity, since that reflects my own approach to my own struggles. Thought it might be a good fit for a game jam about mental health and, well, being 
safe in our world.

Credits:
- Written and Designed by Trainspotter
- Built in Decker, a multimedia sketchpad made by Internet Janitor
- Wiggly Paint and Twine interpreter by Internet Janitor
- Moveable macWindow contraption by Faffotron; many, many others by Internet Janitor
- Lino print by pragmattics on Twitter
- Song by Maya
- Icons by Leo Red
Accidentally (but definitively) inspired by En Abime! Check it out, if you dare >:)
PS: I included editable versions in the downloads in case anybody likes the format of the game and would enjoy experimenting with making interactive fiction of their own using it as a template! Just please credit me if you do so :)
Documentation for using Decker can be found at http://beyondloom.com/decker/

details.title: Exorcize Me
details.description:
--------------------
Your family believes that a "demon" resides in you. Even though you've never believed in that kind of stuff, you can't help but dwell on that idea.
You're taken to get this "demon" exorcized. Who knows, maybe you'll finally be what's considered "normal" to your family. Maybe... you'll be able to stop hiding your true self... if that "self" even survives this.
--------------------
Features:
-Beautiful artwork (including 20+ cgs!)
-Beautiful soundtrack
-A very snarky narrator(we need something to contrast the dread of this one!)
-Around 20-40 minutes of playtime
-A single, inescapable ending
--------------------
Note: This game, like my other games, contains some pretty sensitive content. Player discretion is advised.
Another Note: This game takes place in the same universe as my other games, but you don't need to play them to understand this game!
Created for the 6th RPGMaker Horror Jam, hosted by Beregon and Daybloom Project!
PS: I have the OST and CGs available for purchase, but you don't have to actually buy the pack to get access to them. If you want access, just go into the game files, and dig around in audio and pictures. You can still use them for thumbnails and stuff, even if you haven't purchased the pack!

game.title: Flower Boutique
game.short_text: Manage your own flower boutique and restore colour to your world!

details.title: Flower Boutique
details.description:
Made for the first Safe In Our World Mental Health Game Dev Champions 2024 jam between 1st June & 31st October 2024.
Solo Dev (18-24) Category Entry
Manage your own flower boutique, growing your business and forging relationships to help restore colour to your world!
- Customise your character with purchasable clothing & hairstyles
- Decorate your home & shop with purchasable decor styles
- Manage your flower shop, growing and foraging for stock and gaining upgrades
- Help your customers with their own struggles and collect their mementos
This is the most complex game I've made so far so any feedback & bug reports are much appreciated!Thanks for downloading, to play just unzip the file and run the .exe!

game.title: Lily Adventuresses! Episode 1: She Who Lost Everything but Her Name
game.short_text: An RPG focusing on loss, love, and grief.

details.title: Lily Adventuresses! Episode 1: She Who Lost Everything but Her Name
details.description:
The first in an RPG anthology series featuring women loving women.
Specific category for Safe in Our World Mental Health Game Dev Champions 2024:  [Solo Dev, Ages 25+]
Recognitions for the Entire Series[2025] Nominee, IGG Philippines Indie Community Awards, Excellence in Storytelling[2025] Entry, Philippine GameDev Expo Indie Game Stars[2025] Entry, Southeast Asia RPG Maker Showcase[2025] Finalist, GDAP GameOn Awards Professional Track[2025] Nominee, GDAP GameOn Awards Professional Track, Best Game Narrative[2025] Entry, Southeast Asia Games Onward[2025] Entry, RPG Maker Spotlight
Important NoteIt is HIGHLY recommended to use EasyRPG Player, especially if you are running modern versions of Windows or even non-Windows operating systems. See the site's Downloads section for more details.
StoryJada Roberta Cockrum is the heroine who has defeated the demon lord Calazzelpha and brought peace to the world of Baihaerun, but at the cost of her dear friends and practically everything else but her name.
Five months have passed since that climatic battle, and Jada embarks on another journey to find herself and recover from her loss.
Features
A heartrending and uplifting WLW story about loss, grief, and the will to move onClassic RPG gameplay made possible by RPG Maker 2000Flashbacks that flesh out the characters
Characters

The heroine who, despite having defeated the Demon Lord Calazzelpha alongside her three trusted friends, has lost everything but her name. Fortunately, her rough-and-tumble attitude surprisingly gives her the strength to move forward. She’s also not interested in inheriting her father’s business.

Jada’s childhood friend and classmate. She originally aimed to retire from adventuring and become a geologist after the demon lord is defeated, but her frail body stood in her way. Nevertheless, she had a fiery fighting spirit and an awesome singing voice, that she influenced her friends to train as singers. Succumbed to pneumonia minutes after the decisive battle against the demon lord.

She was once an orphan and distrusted other people, but Jada showed her the way. She is the great antithesis of negativity, cheerful and spreading hope wherever she is. She also has a predilection for “beautiful boys”. She died the morning before the expected victory march due to a stress-induced heart attack.

The only spellcaster in the team. She is capable at offensive and healing magic, as well as more potent exorcist magic used to banish demons. She also has a thing for new technologies, as she hails from an inventor family. After the decisive battle, she went missing and was declared KIA, but Jada believes she’s not dead.

She used to fight for the demon lord, but switched sides once the assassin unit she once belonged to was eliminated and her friend was unjustly slain. She’s at first seriously hostile, but later softens up once Jada knows of her circumstances. She hates being called a dark elf and prefers the term “drow” instead.

Best known as “Virgilia the Quantum Wolf”, she’s a gun-slinging lone wolf, but is not really hostile to others, as she just prefers to work alone in the continuing struggle against evil. As an experienced warrior, she can dispatch enemies with ease, and her gun even converts into a sword whenever the need arises.
CreditsOriginal Concept, Story, Map Design, Event Programming, Planning, and DebuggingBenedict "Moonlight Bomber" Villariaza
 Dedicated to and in Memory ofJayson CruzIbn Larry Fe Barra

🌱 Create your own unique plants by combining a variety of seeds. Every plant is different!

🖱️ Controls
🖱️ Use your mouse to play!
 
👤 About
Small games, big wonders
 
MicroGiant is a brand new hobby project by Joran & Tijmen, two Belgian gamedev study friends. We made "Seeds of Hope" as our first game after graduating in the summer of 2024, and hope to grow more of these genuine experiences in the future.
 
Joran De Pessemier - Code, Design, Story, Sounds

Tijmen Matthys - Art, Design, Music, Plant Code
 

❤️ Thanks for playing and take care!

💬 Comment below to share your thoughts.

💬 Follow us & play our future games!

game.title: Close Knit
game.short_text: Hand-drawn 2D narrative adventure about knitting, magic, & believing in yourself

details.title: Close Knit
details.description:
A cozy narrative adventure platformer we crafted for Safe In Our World Mental Health Game Dev Champions 2024. The theme was to make a game centered around mental health, and we wrote about impostor syndrome, returning to a place you once knew, and learning to create out of love and not for validation.
* * * * *
Use WASD to move, press space to jump, press E to interact, and hold down left click to speed up text. The web build may experience slight lag in certain browsers; consider downloading the executable if you experience any issues with the web build.
Hint: If you get lost, you can always go home and ask Grandma for directions. If you get stuck towards the end of the game, remember to right click in midair to propel yourself with your magic needles!
Known issues: There is currently a collider error in the bakery scene that allows players to walk off the right side of the map and lose control of the character. Thank you to HinmanLantern for pointing out 2 potential softlock spots in the library and sewers (see their comment below for the exact spots)! As this was made for a game jam, we haven't yet implemented save functionality, so you will have to restart the game if this happens. We will fix these issues and add save/reload capability in a post-jam update, so stay tuned!
Our Game Crafters
luxille - narrative, programming, music (main theme)ufolily - character & UI arttinhovercarinice - environment art
wtc654666 - level design, programming
emerald8 - programming
Free-to-use sound effects and additional BGM from Sound Effect Lab and Dova. Special thanks to Wise Fish for inspiring the NPC designs :)For Windows download: Unzip the downloaded file and run Close Knit.exe

details.title: GUS IS SAD
details.description:
"Why am I like this"

GUS IS SAD tells the story of Gus, little alien trying to deal with his depression, and how those around him try to support him.

For an ideal experience, zoom into the webpage and listen to whatever music you feel might be appropriate.

This game was made for the Safe In Our World Mental Health Game Dev Champions 2024. 
The goal of this game is to show depression as close to reality as possible. People dealing with depression are still able to talk with their friends, doing things, having fun. Gus will deal with a variety of situations during his little journey, while having a lot of doubts about his own well-being.

Thanks Internet Janitor for the Turtle Contraption !
Thanks Louis and Akkfor for playtesting the game !

game.title: Life in Small Steps
game.short_text: Follow Clara as she rebuilds her life, small step after small step, with a severe anxiety disorder.

details.title: Life in Small Steps
details.description:
The Game
     
For some people, life is lived in small, cautious steps. Follow Clara as she navigates the consequences of her anxiety disorder on her thinking abilities, her work-life and her relationships. Through her discussions with her therapist and her participation in a scientific study, you will dive into the daily struggles of a person with long-term mental illness that impacts their cognitive abilities.

A note: If you can, download the native version for your OS for a smoother experience. The audio is compressed for the web version. Slower machines might also struggle with the web player.
Accessibility features

We have our heart set on lowering barriers that might stop people from playing or enjoying our game. In that spirit, Life in Small Steps was designed from the start to include a variety of accessibility features:
Play with mouse, keyboard, or controllerPlay the puzzles with symbols with addition to or in place of coloursSet your text speed and sizePlay the dialogues with high contrast textPlay only the narrative parts of the game, or only the puzzlesSimplify animations for visual comfort or slower machinesRemap all controls
Puzzle screen in colour-blind mode
The Team
LaChapeliere - Design and writing (lead), Art (lead), ProgrammingKinoxKlark - Programming (lead), Design, Arta_sett - Music and audio (lead), DesignPurbsi - Voice acting (Psychiatrist), Proofreadinginty - Voice acting (Clara)

Special thanks to our playtesters, who helped us make this game great!
Our project makes use of the Cadman and Paulo Bitencourt fonts.
Our experience with the Safe In Our World jam
This game was created for the "Mental Health Game Dev Champions 2024" jam from Safe In Our World, in the "Small Team 25+" category. This jam had the particularity to be longer than most, with a five-month creation time.

          Some early design workWe took this opportunity to dive into a bigger project, but also to polish our game. We appreciated being able to playtest several times with various people, hunt and solve bugs, and generally do a lot of back-and-forth on all aspects of the game.
As expected, we also encountered our fair share of challenges: mistakenly saving over recorded files, having to deal with a language which wasn't our first, struggling with our own mental health issues...
Life In Small Steps is an autofiction, a game born from personal experience, from one of the creator's life and from collected testimonies, rather than scientific research. It was a choice we felt fit the theme of the game jam, that felt like an invitation to share our stories around mental health.
Life In Small Steps won the "Technical Innovation" category prize of the jam!

game.title: Glass Thoughts
game.short_text: Take the role of a troubled crime investigator, dive into his subconsciousness and face your fears.

details.title: Glass Thoughts
details.description:
Glass thoughts – a downloadable visual novel/point and click game for PCs. 

Content warning
This game contains mature content, including depictions of violence, blood, death, medical malpractice and themes of mental illness. Viewer discretion is advised.

Witold Miller – a young Polish-German prosecutor is investigating a series of tedious cases around the Free City of Gdansk. This tiny city, being a melting point of different cultures and political movements, cannot be considered a safe place in the middle of the roaring 20’s. Soon, Witold realizes that in order to solve the mystery of a murder, he needs to dive into the depths of his own psyche.
Traveling deep into his self-consciousness, Witold confronts the surreal, hostile and distorted world of his fears
This game was created as a submission for “Safe in our world” Game Jam (2024) in the category: team under 24 years old.


Approximate playtime: 2 hours4 unique minigamesOriginal soundtrack and sound effectsMultiple endings based on player choices



Advancing the story further (skipping the dialogue): enter/mouse click left/spacebar
Navigate the dialogue box: left/right arrow
View History Logs – tilde [~]



Tomasz Maligranda – Programmer, Director, Game Designer

Ignacy Kitłowski – Writer, Story Designer, Co-Director

 Ambre Buathier – Lead Character & Prop Designer, Environmental Artist, Additional Art

Jon Kušar– Lead Background Designer, UI Artist, Additional Art

 Nadia Kasprowicz – Illustrator

 Phuwit Sangudomlert – Music & Sounds




Other features:
  
Point&Click minigame – locate the interactive parts and use mouse left click to activate them. You can use the highlight button to show the interactive elements of the surroundings. 
  
Phone Minigame – during the phone minigame there are in-game instructions on how to use the phone. Use the highlight button to mark interactive elements of the phone, instructions, and numbers the character has to call. 
  
Documents minigame – click on the “stamp” circle to mark the document as a suspect. You can mark two documents. In case of an error, you may click on the stamp circle again to remove the stamp and mark another documents.  
  
Endings – there are multiple endings in the game, based on the player’s action. Some of your choices may have consequences…
For more information, open the in-game help page.

details.title: For Closure,
details.description:
"never felt like just a game—it was so much more than that. [...] every ghostly artifact felt incredibly human."

System Requirements

Windows: Windows 10 or newer (64-bit), DirectX 12 compatible graphics card
Mac: macOS 10.14 or newer
Memory: 1 GB RAM minimum

Installation Windows: Download and extract the ZIP file, then run the .exe file. If you see a security warning, click "More info" then "Run anyway."

Mac: Download and extract the ZIP file, then run the .app file. If macOS blocks it (sorry), go to System Preferences > Security & Privacy and click "Open Anyway."

game.title: Grounded
game.short_text: You're living your life as usual. But life suddenly takes a turn for the worse as a global pandemic hits.

details.title: Grounded
details.description:
Set in the time of COVID19 pandemic, the story follows the main character as they struggle to get through their daily life while dealing with their anxiety and fear for the future due to the pandemic. 
Each mini game is based on different grounding techniques and the player will help the main character fight their anxiety and fears by keeping them grounded.
This game is made as a submission for Safe in Our World Mental Health Game Dev Champions 2024 under small team ages 25+ category.
Meet the team:lovely_dru - game design, writer, music, programmerWings-poo - game design, editor, programmerGamerJK8 - artistIgnis Kambing - photographer, photo editor

details.title: To Open the enduring Mind
details.description:
A terrible calamity has occurred, and your mind is clear. Free from doubt, fear and trouble, but also of joy, hope and love. You must build it back up again, from the ground up, or you will have no hope of awakening.
An automation game set inside the mind, with all the aspects of the spirit as characters.
Made in löve2d
Font used: Komikazba by Apostrophic Labs
Portrait for "Love" character by Danah
Music obtained from the Free Music Archive"Initiation" by Semion Krivenko-Adamov"A very minimalist improvisation" by Circus Marcus"Nothing Ever Happened" by Siddharta
Tools used:
Notepad++Paint.netGitOpen Office WriterDesmos Graphing CalculatorIf you are playing on Mac or Linux, you must download love2d from here: https://love2d.org/
Then run the .love file using love2d.
